_note: Worked example: Ironvale Equipment Works, a fictional discrete manufacturer of industrial equipment. Distinctives vs the harborline example: a self_fk part hierarchy (the one example that showcases it), an scd2 standard-cost history on the ERP item master, BOM-driven material consumption derived from work orders x BOM, lot genealogy with a completeness control, case-conditioned yield/defect rates by work center, and an OEE mart. Keys starting with '_' are comments and are stripped before validation.

calendar._note: Plant runs Mon-Fri two shifts with Saturday overtime and a Sunday maintenance window; July dips for summer shutdown, quarter-end months push.

vocab._note: Per-part-type name pools so part names stay coherent with part_type (a finished good never reads like bar stock).
inspection_note: Surface finish marginal on second sample.
Burr found on edge, deburred and passed.
Torque spec verified on all fasteners.
Paint thickness low on one panel.
First article approved by quality engineer.
downtime_note: Tool change took longer than standard.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables.name: control_catalog
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control definitions; break populations come from the control/DQ views.

cases.3: Finished lots tie to completed work orders
cases.5: Cost accounting vs plant operations scrap metric

cases.4: month x plant x product family
cases.5: plant x work center x product family

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.9: asset name drift vs MES machine master
cases.11: work order carries manual quality override
cases.12: MES feed ingested more than 5 days late
cases.13: work center missing cost center mapping
cases.14: conflicting current work center cost mapping

tables._note: PLM part master with a self_fk where-used hierarchy: parent_part_id always points at an EARLIER (lower-id, older) part, so the hierarchy is a DAG by construction. introduced_at is sorted with 70% pre-horizon backfill, so part ids correlate with engineering age and in-window activity does not ramp from zero.
tables.name: part
tables.purpose: Engineering part master across all part types (FG, subassembly, component, raw).

columns.name: part_name
columns._note: part_type is drawn first, then the name comes from that type's pool - finished goods read like machines, raw materials like stock.

columns.name: make_profile
columns._note: Single scale_by driver reused downstream: 'none' for buy parts (no work orders, no BOM), else the part type. Keeps work orders and BOMs off purchased parts without a second conditioning column.

columns.name: parent_part_id
columns._note: self_fk where-used hierarchy; 12% roots (top-level products and free stock).

tables._note: Manufacturing BOM kept in PLM. Generated per assembly part with scale_by on make_profile so only make FG/subassembly/component parts carry BOM lines. Component picks use zipf(0.5): common fasteners and seals appear on many BOMs without one part dominating.
tables.name: bom_component
tables.purpose: Single-level BOM lines per assembly; multi-level structure emerges by chaining through subassembly parts.

columns.name: qty_per
columns._note: Standard per-unit quantities are quantized like real BOMs (1, 2, 4, 8...).

tables._note: ERP item master, 1:1 from PLM via parent_copy, with an scd2 standard-cost history: ~30% of items carry 1-2 predecessor cost versions with chained effective dates (current_flag 0). FK and per_parent pools only ever see current versions.
tables.name: item
tables.purpose: ERP material master with standard cost (scd2 history on cost revisions).

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: Item created shortly after the PLM part is introduced, so backfilled parts have backfilled ERP items.

tables.name: work_center
tables.purpose: Work centers across the two plants; yield, defect rates, cycle times, and labor rates all condition on center_type.

columns.name: established_at
columns._note: All work centers predate the data window (backfill_share 1.0), so machines and maintenance history have sensible vintages.

tables.name: machine
tables.purpose: Shop-floor machines per work center.

columns.name: commissioned_date
columns._note: Offset from the work center's establishment, so machine vintages span 2008-2025 instead of bunching inside the data window.

tables._note: CMMS asset registry mirrors MES machines 1:1; the conflicting_source_values imperfection then makes asset names drift from machine codes, which DQ-009 catches (classic CMMS-vs-MES master drift).
tables.name: asset
tables.purpose: Maintainable asset registry (one asset per machine).

tables._note: Work orders only exist for make parts: scale_by on make_profile zeroes out buy parts and raw materials, weights FG > subassembly > make-component, and a heavy-tailed per_parent_multiplier creates runner products. Quantities are standard lot sizes (1/5/10/25...), not continuous draws.
tables.name: work_order
tables.purpose: Production work orders.
tables.grain: one row per production work order

columns.name: planned_start_date
columns._note: Calendar-weighted, never before the ERP item existed.

tables.name: work_order_event
tables.purpose: Work order status history emitted by the state machine.
tables.grain: one row per work order per state entered

tables._note: Routing operations. Yield is the showcase correlation: scrap_pct is case-conditioned on the work center type (machining ~93.5% first-pass yield, test ~99%), quantities are quantized lot sizes, and labor_cost = qty x constant cycle x constant rate, so exact amounts repeat like real backflush postings. Timestamps are stamped by the mes_operation_schedule derivation INSIDE each work order's actual released->completed window.
tables.name: operation
tables.purpose: Work order routing operations with reported quantities.
tables.grain: one row per work order per routing operation

columns.name: reported_date
columns._note: Anchors the source_stamped trait timestamps near the work order's life.

columns.name: work_center_id
columns._note: Affinity match on plant with a 3% leak (outside-processing at the sister plant).

columns.name: machine_id
columns._note: Machines belong to the operation's work center (2% miscoded scans).

columns.name: scrap_pct
columns._note: First-pass yield 92-99% by work center via case conditioning - the manufacturing realism must.

columns.name: labor_cost
columns._note: Quantized qty x constant cycle x constant rate: real amount columns repeat exact values (duplicate-mass realism check).

tables._note: In-process/final inspections on a share of operations (~40% of work orders end up inspected). Defect counts are case-conditioned on work center type so machining fails ~4x more often than test; disposition only exists for failed samples.
tables.name: inspection
tables.purpose: Quality inspections sampled against routing operations.
tables.grain: one row per inspection sample per operation

columns.name: defects_found
columns._note: Defect rate by work center via case conditioning: machining fails ~22% of samples, test ~5%.

tables.name: nonconformance
tables.purpose: NCRs raised from failed inspections (scrap, supplier returns, and a third of reworks).

tables.name: downtime_event
tables.purpose: Machine downtime intervals from the SCADA feed.
tables.grain: one row per machine downtime interval

tables.name: maintenance_order
tables.purpose: Preventive and corrective maintenance work orders per asset.
tables.grain: one row per maintenance work order

tables.name: maintenance_order_event
tables.purpose: Maintenance order status history emitted by the state machine.
tables.grain: one row per maintenance order per state entered

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations - real lineage. Material issues are BOM x released work orders with deterministic over/under-issue variance aimed at the consumption recon control.
tables.name: material_issue
tables.purpose: Component issues to released work orders, exploded from the BOM.
tables.grain: one row per work order per BOM line issue

tables.name: lot
tables.purpose: Material lots: purchased receipt lots for buy parts plus one produced lot per completed work order.

tables.name: lot_genealogy
tables.purpose: Forward/backward lot trace: produced lot <- consumed component lot, one edge per lot-controlled material issue.

tables.name: plm_item_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly PLM extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: plm_item
tables.purpose: Cleaned PLM parts; normalizes raw extract dates.

tables.name: item_identifier
tables.purpose: Source-system identifier crosswalk for canonical parts (PLM part numbers + ERP item ids).

tables.name: part
tables.purpose: Canonical part joining PLM and ERP through the crosswalk.

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_part
tables.purpose: Part dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_work_center
tables.purpose: Work center dimension.

tables.name: dim_machine
tables.purpose: Machine dimension.

tables.name: fact_production_order
tables.purpose: Production order fact with rolled yield and material cost.
tables.grain: one row per production work order

tables.name: fact_machine_utilization_daily
tables.purpose: Daily machine utilization with OEE components (availability x performance x quality).
tables.grain: one row per machine per active production date

tables.name: fact_operation_event
tables.purpose: Typed MES operation-event fact sourced from the landed/staged MES feed.
tables.grain: one row per MES operation event

tables.name: fact_material_consumption
tables.purpose: Material consumption fact aligned to work-order, component, work-center, and date dimensions.
tables.grain: one row per work-order component issue

tables.name: month_end_wip_snapshot
tables.purpose: SQLite-modeled materialized view for month-end WIP close snapshots.
tables.grain: one row per month-end x plant x product family

tables.name: erp_work_order_extract
tables.purpose: ERP work-order file landing table with verbatim text fields, file ids, batch ids, and ingestion timestamps.

tables.name: erp_work_order
tables.purpose: Typed and deduplicated ERP work-order staging table with guarded date parsing.

tables.name: mes_operation_event
tables.purpose: MES operation-event file landing table with verbatim payload columns and ingestion metadata.

tables.name: mes_operation_event
tables.purpose: Typed and deduplicated MES operation-event staging table with guarded timestamp parsing.

tables.name: cmms_maintenance_extract
tables.purpose: CMMS maintenance extract landing table with file/batch metadata.

tables.name: cmms_maintenance_order
tables.purpose: Typed and deduplicated CMMS maintenance staging table.

tables.name: quality_results_file
tables.purpose: Quality results file landing table with batch metadata and verbatim sample fields.

tables.name: quality_result
tables.purpose: Typed and deduplicated quality-result staging table.

tables.name: work_center_cost_center_mapping
tables.purpose: Human-maintained work-center-to-cost-center mapping spreadsheet used only by cost-accounting marts.

tables.name: mapping_request
tables.purpose: Open workflow queue for unmapped or conflicting work-center cost-center mappings.

tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Code catalog for deployed views plus stored procedures, functions, extracts, and outbound feeds.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Code-level lineage edges from views, procedures, extracts, and feeds to source and target datasets.

tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled integration and warehouse-code jobs tied to cataloged code objects.

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Historical job-run log with successful, partial, and failed runs.

cases.failed: Source file failed checksum or target table lock timed out.
cases.partial: Loaded with rejected rows routed to DQ quarantine.

state_machines.name: work_order_lifecycle
state_machines._note: Right-censored at as_of: recent orders legitimately sit planned/released/in_progress. quality_hold keeps a 16% residual so open holds accumulate (the use-as-is override imperfection feeds the same story).

derivations.name: mes_operation_schedule
derivations._note: Stamps operation start/complete times INSIDE each work order's actual released->completed window (interpolated by op_index with id-math jitter), right-censors ops past as_of, zeroes quantities on not-yet-run ops, then cascades qty_started so each op starts from what survived earlier ops. Uses a helper table + correlated subqueries instead of UPDATE..FROM to stay compatible with older SQLite.
sql: create table tmp_op_schedule (operation_id integer primary key, s_at text, c_at text);
max(julianday(coalesce(completed_at, datetime(started_at, '+' || (2 + work_order_id % 9) || ' days'))) - julianday(started_at), 0.05) as span
from production.work_order where started_at is not null
n as (select work_order_id, count(*) as n_ops from mes.operation group by work_order_id)
started_at = (select case when s.s_at <= '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then s.s_at end from tmp_op_schedule s where s.operation_id = mes.operation.operation_id),
completed_at = (select case when s.c_at <= '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then s.c_at end from tmp_op_schedule s where s.operation_id = mes.operation.operation_id);
update mes.operation set op_status = case when completed_at is not null then 'completed' when started_at is not null then 'in_progress' else 'pending' end;
update mes.operation set qty_started = 0, qty_scrapped = 0, qty_good = 0, labor_cost = 0.0 where op_status = 'pending';
update mes.operation set qty_scrapped = 0, qty_good = 0, labor_cost = 0.0 where op_status = 'in_progress';

qty_started = max(qty_started - coalesce((select sum(o2.qty_scrapped) from mes.operation o2 where o2.work_order_id = mes.operation.work_order_id and o2.op_index < mes.operation.op_index and o2.op_status = 'completed'), 0), qty_scrapped),

qty_good = max(qty_started - coalesce((select sum(o2.qty_scrapped) from mes.operation o2 where o2.work_order_id = mes.operation.work_order_id and o2.op_index < mes.operation.op_index and o2.op_status = 'completed'), 0), qty_scrapped) - qty_scrapped,

labor_cost = round(max(qty_started - coalesce((select sum(o2.qty_scrapped) from mes.operation o2 where o2.work_order_id = mes.operation.work_order_id and o2.op_index < mes.operation.op_index and o2.op_status = 'completed'), 0), qty_scrapped) * cycle_minutes / 60.0 * hourly_rate, 2)

derivations.name: quality_inspection_results
derivations._note: Inspections stamp shortly after their operation completes; samples against not-yet-completed operations stay pending with no result.
sql: from mes.operation o where o.operation_id = quality.inspection.operation_id and o.completed_at is not null);
update quality.inspection set result = 'pending', disposition = 'pending', defects_found = null where inspected_at is null;

derivations.name: inventory_lot_purchased
derivations._note: 3-8 receipt lots per buy part with deterministic id-math dates (Sundays nudged to Monday: receiving is closed).
sql: with recursive seq(n) as (select 1 union all select n + 1 from seq where n < 10)
select p.part_id * 16 + s.n, p.part_id, 'LOT-P' || printf('%07d', p.part_id * 16 + s.n), 'purchased', null,
case when (p.part_id + s.n) % 11 = 0 then 'depleted' when (p.part_id + s.n) % 17 = 0 then 'quality_hold' else 'available' end,
from plm.part p join seq s on s.n <= 3 + (p.part_id % 6)

derivations.name: inventory_lot_produced
derivations._note: Exactly one finished lot per COMPLETED work order (lot-per-work-order genealogy grain); quantity is the last operation's good quantity, so scrapped units never reach stock.
sql: select 500000 + wo.work_order_id, wo.part_id, 'LOT-W' || printf('%07d', wo.work_order_id), 'produced', wo.work_order_id,
coalesce((select o.qty_good from mes.operation o where o.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id and o.completed_at is not null order by o.op_index desc limit 1), wo.qty_planned),
case when wo.status = 'closed' and wo.work_order_id % 5 < 3 then 'consumed' else 'available' end,

derivations.name: production_material_issue
derivations._note: BOM explosion against released work orders. ~8% of issues carry deliberate over/under-issue beyond the 5% tolerance (every 23rd/29th hash bucket) - those are exactly what control_recon_consumption_vs_bom flags. Lot picks rotate deterministically through each component's lots; parts with no lots issue lot-less (bulk/non-lot-controlled).
sql: join plm.bom_component bc on bc.part_id = wo.part_id and bc.component_part_id != wo.part_id
select d.*, round(d.qty_planned * d.qty_per * (1.0 + d.scrap_factor), 2) as qty_std,
select lot_id, part_id, row_number() over (partition by part_id order by lot_id) - 1 as rk,
count(*) over (partition by part_id) as cnt
select row_number() over (order by c.work_order_id, c.bom_line_id),
left join lot_ranked lr on lr.part_id = c.component_part_id and lr.rk = (c.work_order_id + c.line_number) % lr.cnt
left join erp.item i on i.part_id = c.component_part_id and i.current_flag = 1;

derivations.name: inventory_lot_genealogy
derivations._note: One trace edge per lot-carrying issue whose work order produced a finished lot. The missing_xref imperfection later deletes ~2% of edges - DQ-005 finds the resulting trace dead-ends.
sql: pl.lot_id, mi.lot_id, mi.work_order_id, mi.component_part_id, mi.issue_id, mi.qty_issued, date(mi.issued_at)
join inventory.lot pl on pl.work_order_id = mi.work_order_id and pl.lot_type = 'produced'

derivations.name: quality_nonconformance
derivations._note: NCRs from failed inspections: all scrap/supplier-return dispositions plus a third of reworks; ~45-day-old NCRs mostly closed, recent ones open (as-of snapshot realism).
sql: case when q.defects_found >= 4 then 'major' when q.inspection_id % 19 = 0 then 'critical' else 'minor' end,
case when date(q.inspected_at) <= date('2025-12-31', '-45 days') and q.inspection_id % 17 != 0 then 'closed'
case when date(q.inspected_at) <= date('2025-12-31', '-45 days') and q.inspection_id % 17 != 0
then datetime(q.inspected_at, '+' || (3 + (q.inspection_id * 7) % 38) || ' days') else null end

derivations.name: raw_erp_work_order_extract
derivations._note: ERP work-order landing feed: raw text dates, file/batch ids, ingestion timestamp, and replay rows to make staging dedup load-bearing.
sql: cast(wo.work_order_id as text), wo.work_order_number, cast(wo.item_id as text), cast(wo.part_id as text),
wo.plant, wo.order_class, wo.status, cast(wo.qty_planned as text),
case when wo.released_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.released_at) end,
case when wo.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.completed_at) end,
case when wo.closed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.closed_at) end,
cast(wo.work_order_id as text), wo.work_order_number, cast(wo.item_id as text), cast(wo.part_id as text),
wo.plant, wo.order_class, wo.status, cast(wo.qty_planned as text),
case when wo.released_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.released_at) end,
case when wo.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.completed_at) end,
case when wo.closed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', wo.closed_at) end,

derivations.name: stg_erp_work_order
sql: select landing_row_id, cast(source_work_order_id as integer) as work_order_id, work_order_number,
cast(item_id as integer) as item_id, cast(part_id as integer) as part_id, plant, lower(order_class) as order_class,
lower(status) as status, cast(qty_planned_text as integer) as qty_planned,
then substr(planned_start_date_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(planned_start_date_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(planned_start_date_text, 4, 2) end as planned_start_date,
then substr(released_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(released_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(released_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(released_at_text, 11) end as released_at,
then substr(completed_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(completed_at_text, 11) end as completed_at,
then substr(closed_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(closed_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(closed_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(closed_at_text, 11) end as closed_at,
then substr(source_updated_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(source_updated_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(source_updated_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(source_updated_at_text, 11) end as source_updated_at,
select *, row_number() over (partition by work_order_id order by ingested_at desc, landing_row_id desc) as rn
from parsed where work_order_id is not null
select work_order_id, work_order_number, item_id, part_id, plant, order_class, status, qty_planned,
planned_start_date, released_at, completed_at, closed_at, source_updated_at, file_id, batch_id, ingested_at

derivations.name: raw_mes_operation_event
derivations._note: MES operation-event landing feed; replay rows make stg dedup choose the later batch.
sql: cast(o.operation_id as text), cast(o.work_order_id as text), cast(o.op_index as text), cast(o.op_number as text),
o.plant, cast(o.work_center_id as text), cast(o.machine_id as text), o.op_status,
cast(o.qty_started as text), cast(o.qty_good as text), cast(o.qty_scrapped as text), cast(o.labor_cost as text),
case when o.started_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', o.started_at) end,
case when o.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', o.completed_at) end,
cast(o.operation_id as text), cast(o.work_order_id as text), cast(o.op_index as text), cast(o.op_number as text),
o.plant, cast(o.work_center_id as text), cast(o.machine_id as text), o.op_status,
cast(o.qty_started as text), cast(o.qty_good as text), cast(o.qty_scrapped as text), cast(o.labor_cost as text),
case when o.started_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', o.started_at) end,
case when o.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', o.completed_at) end,

derivations.name: stg_mes_operation_event
sql: select landing_row_id, cast(source_operation_id as integer) as operation_id, cast(source_work_order_id as integer) as work_order_id,
cast(op_index_text as integer) as op_index, cast(op_number_text as integer) as op_number, plant,
cast(work_center_id_text as integer) as work_center_id, cast(machine_id_text as integer) as machine_id, lower(op_status) as op_status,
cast(qty_started_text as integer) as qty_started, cast(qty_good_text as integer) as qty_good,
cast(qty_scrapped_text as integer) as qty_scrapped, cast(labor_cost_text as real) as labor_cost,
then substr(started_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(started_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(started_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(started_at_text, 11) end as started_at,
then substr(completed_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(completed_at_text, 11) end as completed_at,
then substr(source_updated_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(source_updated_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(source_updated_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(source_updated_at_text, 11) end as source_updated_at,
select *, row_number() over (partition by operation_id order by ingested_at desc, landing_row_id desc) as rn
from parsed where operation_id is not null
select operation_id, work_order_id, op_index, op_number, plant, work_center_id, machine_id, op_status,
qty_started, qty_good, qty_scrapped, labor_cost, started_at, completed_at, source_updated_at, file_id, batch_id, ingested_at

derivations.name: raw_cmms_maintenance_extract
sql: datetime(mo.requested_date, '+21 hours'), cast(mo.mo_id as text), mo.mo_number, cast(mo.asset_id as text), cast(mo.machine_id as text),
mo.plant, mo.mo_type, mo.priority, mo.status, strftime('%m/%d/%Y', mo.requested_date),
case when mo.approved_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', mo.approved_at) end,
case when mo.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', mo.completed_at) end,
cast(mo.labor_hours as text), cast(mo.parts_cost as text)
cast(mo.mo_id as text), mo.mo_number, cast(mo.asset_id as text), cast(mo.machine_id as text),
mo.plant, mo.mo_type, mo.priority, mo.status, strftime('%m/%d/%Y', mo.requested_date),
case when mo.approved_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', mo.approved_at) end,
case when mo.completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', mo.completed_at) end,
cast(mo.labor_hours as text), cast(mo.parts_cost as text)

derivations.name: stg_cmms_maintenance_order
sql: select landing_row_id, cast(source_mo_id as integer) as mo_id, mo_number, cast(asset_id_text as integer) as asset_id,
cast(machine_id_text as integer) as machine_id, plant, lower(mo_type) as mo_type, lower(priority) as priority, lower(status) as status,
then substr(requested_date_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(requested_date_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(requested_date_text, 4, 2) end as requested_date,
then substr(approved_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(approved_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(approved_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(approved_at_text, 11) end as approved_at,
then substr(completed_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(completed_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(completed_at_text, 11) end as completed_at,
cast(labor_hours_text as real) as labor_hours, cast(parts_cost_text as real) as parts_cost, file_id, batch_id, ingested_at
select *, row_number() over (partition by mo_id order by ingested_at desc, landing_row_id desc) as rn
from parsed where mo_id is not null
select mo_id, mo_number, asset_id, machine_id, plant, mo_type, priority, status, requested_date, approved_at, completed_at,

derivations.name: raw_quality_results_file
sql: datetime(q.recorded_date, '+20 hours'), cast(q.inspection_id as text), q.inspection_number, cast(q.operation_id as text),
cast(q.work_order_id as text), cast(q.work_center_id as text), q.plant, strftime('%m/%d/%Y', q.recorded_date),
cast(q.sample_seq as text), cast(q.sample_size as text), cast(q.defects_found as text), q.result, q.disposition,
case when q.inspected_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', q.inspected_at) end
cast(q.inspection_id as text), q.inspection_number, cast(q.operation_id as text), cast(q.work_order_id as text),
cast(q.work_center_id as text), q.plant, strftime('%m/%d/%Y', q.recorded_date),
cast(q.sample_seq as text), cast(q.sample_size as text), cast(q.defects_found as text), q.result, q.disposition,
case when q.inspected_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', q.inspected_at) end

derivations.name: stg_quality_result
sql: select landing_row_id, cast(source_inspection_id as integer) as inspection_id, inspection_number, cast(operation_id_text as integer) as operation_id,
cast(work_order_id_text as integer) as work_order_id, cast(work_center_id_text as integer) as work_center_id, plant,
then substr(recorded_date_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(recorded_date_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(recorded_date_text, 4, 2) end as recorded_date,
cast(sample_seq_text as integer) as sample_seq, cast(sample_size_text as integer) as sample_size, cast(defects_found_text as integer) as defects_found,
lower(result) as result, lower(disposition) as disposition,
then substr(inspected_at_text, 7, 4) || '-' || substr(inspected_at_text, 1, 2) || '-' || substr(inspected_at_text, 4, 2) || substr(inspected_at_text, 11) end as inspected_at,
select *, row_number() over (partition by inspection_id order by ingested_at desc, landing_row_id desc) as rn
from parsed where inspection_id is not null
select inspection_id, inspection_number, operation_id, work_order_id, work_center_id, plant, recorded_date, sample_seq, sample_size,
defects_found, result, disposition, inspected_at, file_id, batch_id, ingested_at

derivations.name: workflow_mapping_request
derivations._note: Unmapped work centers and duplicate current mappings create workflow cases; cost accounting consumes the mapping, plant ops does not.
sql: when cm.active_rows > 1 then 'conflicting_mapping' end as issue_type
left join current_map cm on cm.source_work_center_id = wc.work_center_id
select row_number() over (order by work_center_id), 'work_center_cost_center', work_center_id, work_center_code, plant,
issue_type, case when issue_type = 'conflicting_mapping' then 'high' else 'medium' end,

derivations.name: raw_plm_item_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the format_drift imperfection later corrupts a slice, which DQ-004 catches.
sql: select cast(part_id as text), part_number, part_type, product_family, part_name, lifecycle_status,

derivations.name: stg_plm_item
sql: select cast(part_id as integer), part_number, lower(part_type), lower(product_family), trim(part_name), lower(lifecycle_status),

derivations.name: xref_item_identifier
derivations._note: Both systems mapped: a crosswalk covering a single system is not a crosswalk. ERP rows only map current scd2 versions.
sql: select p.part_id, 'part', p.part_id, 'CrucibleWorks PLM', 'part', cast(p.part_id as text), date(p.introduced_at), null, 1
select 100000 + row_number() over (order by i.item_id), 'part', i.part_id, 'ForgeLine ERP', 'item', cast(i.item_id as text), date(i.created_at), null, 1

derivations.name: core_part
sql: select s.part_id, s.part_number, s.part_name, s.part_type, s.product_family, s.lifecycle_status,
(select i.make_buy from erp.item i where i.part_id = s.part_id and i.current_flag = 1 limit 1),
(select cast(x.source_record_id as integer) from xref.item_identifier x
where x.canonical_part_id = s.part_id and x.source_system = 'ForgeLine ERP' and x.active_flag = 1 limit 1),
(select i.standard_cost from erp.item i where i.part_id = s.part_id and i.current_flag = 1 limit 1)

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_part
sql: select part_uid, part_number, part_name, part_type, product_family, lifecycle_status, make_buy, standard_cost from core.part;

derivations.name: wh_dim_work_center
sql: select work_center_id, work_center_code, center_type, plant, hourly_rate, shift_pattern from erp.work_center;

derivations.name: wh_dim_machine
sql: select machine_id, machine_code, work_center_id, center_type, plant, manufacturer, commissioned_date, rated_speed_uph, active_flag from mes.machine;

derivations.name: wh_fact_operation_event
sql: select s.operation_id, s.work_order_id, wo.part_id, s.work_center_id, s.machine_id, s.plant,
s.op_index, s.op_number, s.op_status, s.qty_started, s.qty_good, s.qty_scrapped, s.labor_cost,
case when s.started_at is not null and s.completed_at is not null
join stg.erp_work_order wo on wo.work_order_id = s.work_order_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_material_consumption
sql: row_number() over (partition by work_order_id order by op_index, operation_id) as rn
select mi.issue_id, mi.work_order_id, mi.part_id, mi.component_part_id, fw.work_center_id, wo.plant,
case when mi.unit_std_cost is not null then round((mi.qty_issued - mi.qty_standard) * mi.unit_std_cost, 2) end
join stg.erp_work_order wo on wo.work_order_id = mi.work_order_id
left join first_wc fw on fw.work_order_id = mi.work_order_id and fw.rn = 1;

derivations.name: wh_fact_production_order
sql: select wo.work_order_id, wo.work_order_number, wo.part_id, wo.plant, wo.order_class, wo.part_type,
coalesce((select o.qty_good from mes.operation o where o.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id and o.completed_at is not null order by o.op_index desc limit 1), 0),
coalesce((select sum(o.qty_scrapped) from mes.operation o where o.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id and o.completed_at is not null), 0),

case when wo.completed_at is not null then round(1.0 * coalesce((select o.qty_good from mes.operation o where o.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id and o.completed_at is not null order by o.op_index desc limit 1), 0) / wo.qty_planned, 4) end,
case when wo.completed_at is not null and wo.released_at is not null then round(julianday(wo.completed_at) - julianday(wo.released_at), 2) end,
(select round(sum(mi.extended_cost), 2) from production.material_issue mi where mi.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id)

derivations.name: wh_fact_machine_utilization_daily
derivations._note: OEE components per machine-day: availability from SCADA downtime against a 960-minute two-shift day, quality from reported good/total units, performance as a deterministic hash in the 0.62-0.89 band (ideal cycle times are not modeled). oee always recomputes exactly as a*p*q.
sql: select o.machine_id, date(o.started_at) as d, sum(o.qty_started) as units_total, sum(o.qty_good) as units_good
where o.completed_at is not null and o.machine_id is not null
select machine_id, date(started_at) as d, sum(duration_minutes) as dt_min
from mes.downtime_event group by machine_id, date(started_at)
round(0.62 + ((od.machine_id * 31 + cast(strftime('%j', od.d) as integer) * 7) % 28) / 100.0, 4) as performance,
round(case when od.units_total > 0 then 1.0 * od.units_good / od.units_total else 1.0 end, 4) as quality_rate
join mes.machine m on m.machine_id = od.machine_id
left join dtm on dtm.machine_id = od.machine_id and dtm.d = od.d
select row_number() over (order by machine_id, d),
machine_id, work_center_id, plant, d, planned_minutes, downtime_minutes,

derivations.name: nv_production_order_operations
sql: select po.work_order_id, po.work_order_number, po.plant, po.status, po.order_class,
po.qty_planned as order_qty_planned, po.qty_good as order_qty_good, po.qty_scrapped as order_qty_scrapped,
po.actual_material_cost, p.part_key, p.part_number, p.part_name, p.part_type, p.product_family, p.standard_cost,
oe.qty_started, oe.qty_good, oe.qty_scrapped, oe.labor_cost, oe.started_at, oe.completed_at, oe.cycle_time_minutes,
join wh.dim_part p on p.part_key = po.part_key
left join wh.fact_operation_event oe on oe.work_order_id = po.work_order_id
left join wh.dim_work_center wc on wc.work_center_key = oe.work_center_key
left join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = coalesce(oe.operation_date, po.planned_start_date);

derivations.name: nv_material_consumption
sql: parent.part_number as assembly_part_number, parent.part_name as assembly_part_name, parent.product_family, parent.part_type as assembly_part_type,
comp.part_number as component_part_number, comp.part_name as component_part_name, comp.part_type as component_part_type,
mc.qty_standard, mc.qty_issued, mc.unit_std_cost, mc.extended_cost, mc.usage_variance_qty, mc.usage_variance_value,
join wh.dim_part parent on parent.part_key = mc.part_key
join wh.dim_part comp on comp.part_key = mc.component_part_key
left join wh.dim_work_center wc on wc.work_center_key = mc.work_center_key
left join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = mc.issue_date;

derivations.name: nv_machine_utilization
sql: select f.util_id, f.activity_date as production_date, f.plant,
join wh.dim_machine m on m.machine_key = f.machine_key
join wh.dim_work_center w on w.work_center_key = f.work_center_key
join wh.dim_date d on d.date_key = f.activity_date;

derivations.name: bv_oee_daily
sql: select production_date, plant, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type,
sum(planned_minutes) as planned_minutes, sum(runtime_minutes) as runtime_minutes, sum(downtime_minutes) as downtime_minutes,
sum(units_total) as units_total, sum(units_good) as units_good,
round(avg(availability), 4) as availability, round(avg(performance), 4) as performance,
round(avg(quality_rate), 4) as quality_rate, round(avg(oee), 4) as oee
group by production_date, plant, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type;

derivations.name: bv_yield_by_routing
sql: select plant, product_family, part_type, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type, op_number,
count(distinct work_order_id) as work_orders_touched, count(operation_id) as operations_completed,
sum(qty_started) as units_started, sum(qty_good) as units_good, sum(qty_scrapped) as units_scrapped,
where operation_id is not null and op_status = 'completed'
group by plant, product_family, part_type, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type, op_number;

derivations.name: bv_wip_rollforward
sql: select work_order_id, plant, product_family, part_type, planned_start_date, status,
max(order_qty_planned) as qty_planned, max(order_qty_good) as qty_good, max(order_qty_scrapped) as qty_scrapped,
max(coalesce(standard_cost, 0)) as standard_cost, max(coalesce(actual_material_cost, 0)) as material_cost
group by work_order_id, plant, product_family, part_type, planned_start_date, status
select date(o.planned_start_date, 'start of month', '+1 month', '-1 day') as month_end_date,
o.plant, o.product_family, o.status, o.qty_good, o.qty_scrapped, o.standard_cost, o.material_cost, coalesce(l.labor_cost, 0) as labor_cost
from order_one o left join labor_by_order l on l.work_order_id = o.work_order_id
sum(case when status not in ('closed', 'cancelled') then 1 else 0 end) as open_work_orders,
round(sum(case when status not in ('closed', 'cancelled') then (material_cost + labor_cost) * 0.35 else 0 end), 2) as beginning_wip_value,
round(sum(case when status in ('completed', 'closed') then qty_good * standard_cost else 0 end), 2) as completion_credit_value,
round(sum(case when status not in ('closed', 'cancelled') then (material_cost + labor_cost) * 0.35 else 0 end)
- sum(case when status in ('completed', 'closed') then qty_good * standard_cost else 0 end)

derivations.name: mv_month_end_wip_snapshot
sql: select row_number() over (order by month_end_date, plant, product_family),
month_end_date, plant, product_family, open_work_orders, beginning_wip_value, material_issued_value,

derivations.name: mart_costaccounting_scrap_summary
sql: select m.*, row_number() over (partition by source_work_center_id order by effective_start_date desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
sum(b.operations_completed) as operations_completed, sum(b.units_started) as units_started,
sum(b.units_scrapped) as units_scrapped, round(sum(b.scrap_standard_cost), 2) as scrap_absorption_value,
left join ranked_mapping rm on rm.source_work_center_id = b.work_center_key and rm.rn = 1
group by b.plant, b.product_family, b.work_center_key, b.work_center_code, coalesce(rm.cost_center_code, 'UNMAPPED');

derivations.name: mart_costaccounting_wip_close
sql: (select count(*) from workflow.mapping_request r where r.status = 'open' and r.plant = m.plant) as open_mapping_requests

derivations.name: mart_plantops_yield_summary
sql: select plant, product_family, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type,
sum(operations_completed) as operations_completed, sum(units_started) as units_started,
sum(units_good) as units_good, sum(units_scrapped) as units_scrapped,
group by plant, product_family, work_center_key, work_center_code, center_type;

derivations.name: mart_plantops_oee_daily
sql: select production_date, plant, work_center_code, center_type, machines_active, planned_minutes, runtime_minutes,
downtime_minutes, units_total, units_good, availability, performance, quality_rate, oee

derivations.name: control_recon_scrap_metric_discrepancy
derivations._note: Cost accounting uses standard-cost-weighted scrap and the manual cost-center mapping; plant ops uses unit scrap by work center. The control makes that definition drift visible.
sql: case when c.cost_center_code = 'UNMAPPED' then 'mapping_gap' else 'metric_definition_gap' end as break_type
on c.plant = p.plant and c.product_family = p.product_family and c.work_center_key = p.work_center_key

derivations.name: mart_oee_daily
sql: join wh.dim_work_center w on w.work_center_key = f.work_center_key
group by f.activity_date, w.work_center_code, w.center_type, f.plant;

derivations.name: mart_yield_by_work_center
sql: join erp.work_center w on w.work_center_id = o.work_center_id

derivations.name: control_recon_consumption_vs_bom
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections: standard demand recomputed from the BOM (never trusting denormalized copies), actual from issues including restatement reversal pairs. Breaks = designed over/under-issues plus restatement drift.
sql: join plm.bom_component bc on bc.part_id = wo.part_id and bc.component_part_id != wo.part_id
select work_order_id, component_part_id, sum(qty_issued) as issued_qty, avg(unit_std_cost) as unit_cost
case when a.issued_qty > s.standard_qty then 'over_issue' else 'under_issue' end as break_type
join act a on a.work_order_id = s.work_order_id and a.component_part_id = s.component_part_id

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'active item missing standard cost' as issue, cast(item_id as text) as entity_id
from erp.item where current_flag = 1 and standard_cost is null
select 'DQ-002', 'operation references unknown machine', cast(o.operation_id as text)
where o.machine_id is not null and not exists (select 1 from mes.machine m where m.machine_id = o.machine_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'part missing source crosswalk coverage', cast(s.part_id as text)
where (select count(distinct x.source_system) from xref.item_identifier x
select 'DQ-004', 'unparseable raw PLM extract date', part_id
from raw.plm_item_extract where introduced_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-005', 'lot-controlled issue missing genealogy edge', cast(mi.issue_id as text)
where mi.lot_id is not null and mi.adjustment_note is null
and exists (select 1 from inventory.lot pl where pl.work_order_id = mi.work_order_id and pl.lot_type = 'produced')
and not exists (select 1 from inventory.lot_genealogy g where g.source_issue_id = mi.issue_id)
select 'DQ-006', 'negative operation cycle time', cast(operation_id as text)
from mes.operation where completed_at is not null and started_at is not null and completed_at < started_at
select 'DQ-007', 'duplicate downtime delivery', cast(machine_id as text) || '@' || started_at
from mes.downtime_event group by machine_id, started_at having count(*) > 1
select 'DQ-008', 'duplicate inspection entry', cast(operation_id as text) || '#' || cast(sample_seq as text)
from quality.inspection group by operation_id, sample_seq having count(*) > 1
select 'DQ-009', 'asset name drift vs MES machine master', cast(a.asset_id as text)
from cmms.asset a join mes.machine m on m.machine_id = a.machine_id
select 'DQ-010', 'BOM component equals its assembly', cast(bom_line_id as text)
select 'DQ-011', 'work order carries manual quality override', cast(work_order_id as text)
select 'DQ-012', 'MES feed ingested more than 5 days late', cast(operation_id as text)
select 'DQ-013', 'work center missing cost-center mapping', cast(work_center_id as text)
select 'DQ-014', 'conflicting current work-center cost mapping', cast(work_center_id as text)
select 'DQ-015', 'unparseable manufacturing raw feed date', source_work_order_id
from raw.erp_work_order_extract where planned_start_date_text not like '__/__/____';

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Views self-register from sqlite_master; non-SQLite procedures/functions/extracts are cataloged as source text with job history and lineage.
sql: select row_number() over (order by name), name, 'view', 'sqlite', sql,
when name like 'mart_plantops_%' or name like 'bv_oee%' then 'plant_operations'
when name like 'control_%' then 'finance_controls' else 'data_platform' end,
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';
'create procedure proc_standard_cost_roll as update erp.item set standard_cost = standard_cost where current_flag = 1;',
'cost_accounting', '2025-01-03 09:00:00', '2025-12-01 02:00:00', 'deployed', 0, 'Runs after supplier reprices and ECO releases.'
'create procedure proc_period_end_wip_close as insert into mv.month_end_wip_snapshot select * from bv_wip_rollforward;',

'delete from mv.month_end_wip_snapshot; insert into mv.month_end_wip_snapshot select row_number() over (order by month_end_date, plant, product_family), month_end_date, plant, product_family, open_work_orders, beginning_wip_value, material_issued_value, labor_absorbed_value, scrap_value, completion_credit_value, ending_wip_value, ''2025-12-31 23:30:00'' from bv_wip_rollforward;',
'create function fn_shift_calendar_bucket(ts timestamp, work_center_id int) returns varchar as select shift_pattern from erp.work_center where work_center_id = work_center_id;',
'plant_operations', '2025-02-10 11:00:00', '2025-11-15 08:00:00', 'deployed', 0, 'Used by downstream warehouse targets outside SQLite.'

'select l.vendor_code, n.disposition, count(*) as ncr_count from quality.nonconformance n join production.material_issue mi on mi.work_order_id = n.work_order_id join inventory.lot l on l.lot_id = mi.lot_id group by l.vendor_code, n.disposition;',
'select month_end_date, plant, product_family, ending_wip_value from mv.month_end_wip_snapshot where month_end_date = (select max(month_end_date) from mv.month_end_wip_snapshot);',
'finance_systems', '2025-03-01 07:30:00', '2025-12-31 05:00:00', 'deployed', 0, 'Finance feed consumed by the GL close process.'

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
sql: select row_number() over (order by object_name), object_name, 'sqlite_master', object_name, 'defines', 'View source self-registered from sqlite_master.'
select 5000, 'proc_standard_cost_roll', 'erp.item', 'erp.item', 'updates', 'Rolls current standard costs after reprice/ECO approval.'
union all select 5001, 'proc_period_end_wip_close', 'bv_wip_rollforward', 'mv.month_end_wip_snapshot', 'writes', 'Closes period WIP into a snapshot table.'
union all select 5003, 'fn_shift_calendar_bucket', 'erp.work_center', 'bv_oee_daily', 'supports', 'Supplies shift-calendar bucketing to OEE reporting.'
union all select 5004, 'extract_supplier_quality_monthly', 'quality.nonconformance', 'supplier_quality_sftp', 'extracts', 'Outbound supplier-quality defect extract.'
union all select 5005, 'feed_finance_wip_journal', 'mv.month_end_wip_snapshot', 'finance_wip_journal_feed', 'feeds', 'Month-end WIP balance feed to finance.'
union all select 5006, 'raw_erp_work_order_extract_load', 'production.work_order', 'raw.erp_work_order_extract', 'lands', 'ERP work-order extract landing.'
union all select 5008, 'raw_cmms_maintenance_extract_load', 'cmms.maintenance_order', 'raw.cmms_maintenance_extract', 'lands', 'CMMS maintenance extract landing.'
union all select 5009, 'raw_quality_results_file_load', 'quality.inspection', 'raw.quality_results_file', 'lands', 'Quality result file landing.';

imperfections.name: asset_name_drift
imperfections.scenario: CMMS asset names drift from the MES machine master after ad-hoc renames; DQ-009 compares the two registries.

imperfections.name: op_machine_orphans
imperfections.scenario: Retired machines hard-deleted from MES while old operation records still point at them; DQ-002 catches the dangling scans.

imperfections.name: mes_feed_lag
imperfections.scenario: MES backflush batches sync days late after network outages; DQ-012 flags ingestion lag over 5 days.

imperfections.name: plm_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy PLM extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-004 flags unparseable rows.

imperfections.name: item_xref_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Crosswalk rows lost during the 2024 integration-hub migration; DQ-003 reports parts without full source coverage.

imperfections.name: genealogy_edge_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Bulk/backflushed components skip lot scans, so trace edges are missing; DQ-005 (control CTL-002) finds recall-trace dead ends.

imperfections.name: consumption_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Issue corrections posted as reversal + restated pairs; the net drift shows up in the consumption-vs-BOM recon.

imperfections.name: duplicate_inspection_entries
imperfections.scenario: Inspectors re-key results after tablet sync failures; DQ-008 finds doubled samples per operation.

imperfections.name: wo_use_as_is_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Use-as-is dispositions signed off by supervisors; DQ-011 keeps the audit queue.

imperfections.name: item_cost_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Standard costs never set after ECO re-releases; DQ-001 flags costless items and issues lose extended cost downstream.

imperfections.name: scada_duplicate_feeds
imperfections.scenario: SCADA gateway retries re-deliver downtime intervals; DQ-007 finds same-machine same-timestamp doubles before they understate OEE.

imperfections.name: mes_clock_skew
imperfections.scenario: Operator batch entry and PLC clock skew swap start times between adjacent operations; DQ-006 reports negative cycle times.

controls.rule_name: Material consumption ties to BOM standard
controls.expected_break_rate: ~8% of work-order components (designed over/under-issues + restatement drift)

controls.rule_name: Finished lots tie to completed work orders
controls.expected_break_rate: 0 (structural: produced lots derive from completed orders)
~1% at close from late postings and manual overrides
expected definition differences plus unmapped work centers

dq_rules.condition: every part mapped in both PLM and ERP
one interval per machine per start time
one entry per operation per sample
asset name matches MES machine code
ingested within 5 days of source update
live work centers have an approved current cost-center mapping
one current approved mapping per work center
landed work-order dates parse as MM/DD/YYYY

dataflows.name: item_mastering
dataflows.sequence: plm.part -> raw extract -> staging -> xref -> core.part -> wh.dim_part

dataflows.name: plan_to_produce
dataflows.sequence: erp.item -> production.work_order -> raw ERP extract -> stg.erp_work_order -> state machine -> mes.operation schedule -> wh.fact_production_order

dataflows.name: material_and_genealogy
dataflows.sequence: plm.bom_component x released work orders -> production.material_issue -> inventory.lot (produced) -> inventory.lot_genealogy

dataflows.name: machine_to_oee
dataflows.sequence: mes.operation -> raw MES events -> stg.mes_operation_event -> wh.fact_operation_event + wh.fact_machine_utilization_daily -> nv/bv OEE views -> mart_plantops_oee_daily

dataflows.name: quality_loop
dataflows.sequence: quality.inspection -> raw quality results -> stg.quality_result -> quality.nonconformance -> supplier quality extract

dataflows.name: cost_accounting_close
